## Social Media Manager
Club L London is the next-generation online fashion retailer for the forward-thinking woman. Conceptualised and crafted in-house and abroad, we specialise in accessible luxury and unique designs of unrivalled quality to flatter all figures. From prom to occasion, am to pm, maternity, bridal and more, we deliver an engaging customer experience that connects our global community of diverse consumers, international fashion influencers and content creators with new and exciting collections dropping each week.
**The Role**
This position can be based at our headquarters in Manchester, UK or Dubai, UAE.
The Social Media Manager will play a pivotal role in developing and leading Club L London’s global campaign and social strategy, managing and overseeing the social and content team. Working closely with the Head of Marketing and Brand Director, you will help define overall strategy while delivering best-in-class campaign rollouts across all social touchpoints, maximising reach, engagement, and impact, and driving the Club L brand vision forward.
Immersed in social trends, fashion, and platform innovation, you will continuously develop fresh, creative ideas that keep Club L industry-leading. You will thrive in a fast-paced environment, collaborate cross-functionally, and communicate clearly. With a creative, solution-focused mindset, you will engage in cultural conversations, respond quickly to trends, and use social listening to gain deeper audience insight.
**Roles and Responsibilities**
· Develop and deliver the brand’s social media strategy, setting clear KPIs relevant to each channel aligned to brand and commercial objectives, including follower growth, engagement, and reach.
· Lead social activity across key platforms including Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, Pinterest, YouTube, and Snapchat, while continuously identifying new and emerging opportunities.
· Conceptualise innovative, social-first concepts that compliment Club L’s wider campaigns, building on the initial concept but giving them a social first approach. Mapping out these campaigns across each social channel in line with the brand vision and campaign strategy and managing end to end.
· Monitor, analyse, and report on social performance using tools such as Google Analytics and native platform insights, identifying successes and areas for optimisation.
· Research benchmarks, customer behaviour, platform developments, digital technologies, and competitor activity to inform planning, maximise ROI, and keep the brand at the forefront.
· Identify and implement new ideas and growth initiatives to drive follower growth, develop brand awareness, increase reach among our community and engagement per post.
· Maximise trading and growth opportunities across international territories.
· Lead the creation of reactive, trend-led content in response to cultural and viral moments, ensuring fast and effective execution through internal creation and outreach.
· Source, develop, and deliver commercially driven brand partnerships.
· Work closely with Outreach, Brand, PR, Customer Services, and wider teams to deliver consistent, high-quality content.
· Act as a brand guardian across all social channels, ensuring a consistent tone of voice aligned with brand guidelines.
· Collaborate cross-functionally with Marketing, Creative, Merchandising, and E-commerce teams.
· Lead and develop the social media team, setting clear objectives, development plans, and processes.
**More about you**
· Proven experience developing and executing social strategies focused on brand awareness, community building, engagement, and follower growth.
· Strong experience delivering high-impact campaign rollouts across multiple social touchpoints.
· Highly creative with a strong aesthetic eye and a clear passion for fashion, culture, and social media.
· Comfortable operating at strategic, tactical, and operational levels.
· Strong project and campaign management skills, underpinned by an analytical approach to performance metrics.
· Excellent editing skills across short-form video, Reels, and production apps.
· Collaborative, proactive, creative thinker with the ability to build strong relationships
· Experience within premium brands or agency environments preferred.
· Proven experience managing and developing teams, with strong workflow and process implementation.
· Excellent communication skills with an inspiring, inclusive leadership style.
* Bi-annual bonus scheme
* 25 days of annual leave (plus bank holidays)
* Extra day off for your birthday
* Flexible working hours around core hours of 10-4
* Cycle to work scheme
* 40% staff discount across Club L and Lavish Alice products
* Healthcare Cashplan
* Free onsite gym
* Enhanced pension contribution
* Enhanced maternity and sick pay
* Free snacks, drinks & treats
* Social events

### Minimum qualifications:

* Master's degree in Computer Science or related technical field.

### About the job
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
The Platforms and Devices team encompasses Google's various computing software platforms across environments (desktop, mobile, applications), as well as our first party devices and services that combine the best of Google AI, software, and hardware. Teams across this area research, design, and develop new technologies to make our user's interaction with computing faster and more seamless, building innovative experiences for our users around the world.
### Responsibilities
* Deliver high impact projects for Home Infrastructure.
* Write clean generalizable code and help promote a culture of pragmatic support for the teams who use our platform.
* Work collaboratively and help support our supportive team environment.
* Work collaboratively with other teams in Home Infrastructure, in Zurich (ZRH), for delivering projects.

Vice President, Software Engineering
Overview: Who is Mastercard? Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. We are seeking a visionary and innovative Vice President of Software Engineering to lead our engineering team focused on building cutting-edge real-time payments (RTP) technology. As the VP, you will have deep product development experience, demonstrating a profound understanding of crafting high-quality, scalable products. Your expertise in platform and domain-driven development will be pivotal in driving our technology strategies. A key aspect of your leadership will be your alignment with the Mastercard Way culture, embodying our core values of creating value together, at speed. Role Overview Oversee the management of the Software Engineering function and act as the highest authority on high-level and complex decisions within the function. Work with business/product owners to develop and deliver new services, introducing new products and bundles. Work on complex enterprise-wide initiatives spanning multiple services/programs and drive resolution. Emulate and drive Mastercard Way behaviour’s through your actions, recognitions, coaching, and employee engagement. Manage and optimize budgets, forecasting, and cost allocation while delivering on business needs. Provide strategic thinking and leadership on a wide range of application types, architectures, and software development methodologies. Benchmark and drive engineering productivity, quality, and technology policy compliance in your areas of ownership. Update Executive Leadership on the status of high-visibility initiatives. Lead and mentor a team of engineering managers and developers. Foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Oversee recruitment, training, and development of engineering staff. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or higher in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field. 8+ years of experience in leading and managing software engineering functions, preferably for software platforms or products in the financial services sector. 5+ years of experience in leading and managing engineering teams, preferably in a distributed and agile environment. Proven track record of delivering high-quality and value-added software platforms or products, as well as ensuring alignment and collaboration with the product, engineering, and customer success teams. Strong knowledge and experience in quality strategy, metrics, standards, and best practices, as well as quality tools, methods, and techniques, such as testing, debugging, troubleshooting, reporting, and documenting. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify and evaluate quality issues and risks, and propose and implement effective and efficient solutions. Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to communicate and influence effectively at all levels of the organization and with external parties. Strong leadership and management skills, with the ability to inspire, motivate, and empower the quality team, as well as foster a quality culture and mindset across the organization. Customer-centric and results-oriented mindset, with the ability to understand and anticipate customer needs and expectations, and deliver value and satisfaction. Flexible and adaptable, with the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced and dynamic environment, and manage multiple and competing priorities and demands
Corporate Security Responsibility
All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:
Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.
